result.headline: Strong fit—healthcare SaaS, AWS, TypeScript, DevOps, and regulated-industry experience.

reasons.detail: Built Sample Assist's drug-testing platform from zero on AWS, aligned with Australian Privacy Act and My Health Records Act requirements—direct healthcare compliance precedent.
5+ years production cloud work; built 20+ NestJS services in TypeScript at Sample Assist, integrated React across the product suite, all shipping to real users.
Designed and provisioned VPC, EKS, RDS Postgres, ALB, IAM in Terraform; integrated AWS Secrets Manager; cut 30% spend through right-sizing—exactly the hands-on AWS scope the JD describes.

reasons.point: DevOps + modern practices embedded in delivery
reasons.detail: Built GitOps pipeline (GitHub Actions + Argo CD), multi-env Helm deploys, Prometheus + Grafana observability, mobile CI/CD with Fastlane—DevOps is how I ship, not a separate function.

reasons.point: AI-assisted development as a working practice
reasons.detail: Shipped Startiny solo to the App Store using Claude Code for implementation; actively use AI in production workflows, not just theory.

relevantSkills: AWS EKS, ECS / Fargate, EC2, ALB / AWS Load Balancer Controller, RDS Postgres
GitHub Actions, Argo CD, GitOps workflows
IAM least-privilege, Encryption at rest / in transit
result.gaps: Haven't shipped React (web) as a primary platform—Sample Assist is mobile-first, so my React depth is narrower than the JD might expect.
result.closer: Happy to chat through how Sample Assist's healthcare + AWS foundation maps to Magentus—let's find a time.

result.headline: Strong fundamentals + AI-assisted shipping; ramp needed on Go, Azure, NextJS stack.

reasons.detail: Shipped Startiny to App Store in 2 weeks using Claude Code for most implementation, including scope/product decisions—directly matches their LLM coding tools requirement.
5+ years shipping NestJS / TypeScript services at Sample Assist and GGJungle; strong fundamentals transfer to their NextJS stack.
Startiny required directing AI, making scope cuts, and iterating through App Store review—exactly the 'product builder' mindset they're asking for.
Led Firestore → RDS Postgres replatform (100+ tables) and built the Coupon module with thoughtful schema; MongoDB is adjacent.
Master's in CS + 5 years production work + shipped solo with AI means I can ramp quickly on Go and Azure.

result.gaps: Go, NextJS, and Azure are not in my production shipping stack—I'd be ramping on the backend language and primary cloud platform.
result.closer: Happy to chat about how the AI-assisted shipping and product-builder mindset translate, and how quickly I'd ramp on Go and Azure.

result.headline: Strong full-stack + DevOps fit; NestJS/TypeScript/React/AWS all direct experience.

reasons.point: NestJS / TypeScript / Node.js production at scale
reasons.detail: Built 20+ NestJS services at Sample Assist with TypeORM, shared modules, and production Postgres — exact stack match for your backend services.
Built and shipped React Native (Expo) for Startiny and contributed frontend work at Sample Assist; comfortable with React and TypeScript.
Designed and provisioned production AWS (EKS, RDS Postgres, Redis, S3, ALB) in Terraform; can own both backend services and infrastructure.
Shipped Startiny to iOS App Store in two weeks using Claude Code; comfortable with AI-assisted workflows, code review, and shipping quality alongside LLM tooling.
Owned feature modules end-to-end at GGJungle (Coupon, VNPAY) and Sample Assist; comfortable with cross-functional squads, product impact, and business outcomes.

result.gaps: PHP/Laravel isn't my primary stack (I shipped NestJS over Node, not Laravel) — I've done production PHP feature work at GGJungle but haven't architected a Laravel codebase; ramp would be straightforward given TypeScript/NestJS fundamentals.
result.closer: This hits most of my core stack directly. Happy to chat about the NestJS/TypeScript alignment and how AI-assisted shipping could fit your product velocity.

result.headline: Strong fundamentals, but frontend React/Next isn't my primary production stack.

reasons.detail: Built NestJS backend at Sample Assist, but frontend React/Next experience is limited to the Startiny side project, not production work at scale.
GitHub Actions, Argo CD, GitOps, Helm — exactly what a modern fintech stack needs, and I've shipped this in production.
Designed and built 20+ NestJS services with shared modules for auth and data access; comfortable with service boundaries and BFF patterns.
Mentored 3 engineers on NestJS/Terraform/Helm standards at Sample Assist; led Firebase → AWS replatform decision-making with one other engineer.
Japanese client work at GGJungle required rigorous specs and documentation; solo shipped Startiny end-to-end; comfortable translating between engineering and stakeholders.

result.gaps: React and Next.js are not my primary production stack — Startiny is React Native on mobile, not web React, and my backend focus at Sample Assist/GGJungle means frontend isn't where I've driven most decisions. I'd be ramping on the modern web frontend ecosystem alongside the role.
result.closer: Open to chat about the backend + DevOps layer and how I'd approach levelling up the frontend piece — happy to jump on a call.
